ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
Off Calendar Jan-20-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Continued To Jan-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Curran appeared by zoom for Platte River Insurance Company.

The Court stated that it is having trouble understanding why Mr. Curran wants to be discharged when the special letters expired in 2018. Mr. Curran stated that he wants the bond to be exonerated. The Court stated that there is a six-year statute of limitation to bring a legal action against the surety company and that it runs out on January 15, 2021. Mr. Curran stated that there are no claims, that the principal has been removed and that he is not aware of any other matters before the Court to prevent exoneration of the bond. The Court stated that it is not comfortable with the current request and that it will continue the matter to a date after January 15, 2021. The Petition for Approval of Final Report of Temporary Special Administrator, etc. is continued to January 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that if no claims are made, the petition will probably be granted at that time.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Tax Return Status
Off Calendar Jun-12-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Curran

Mr. Curran made reference to his February 20, 2018 filing of declaration in regards to status of tax returns, stated that IRS confirmed no tax returns were filed after 2015, he does not know what was filed prior to 2015 since he does not have authority as special administrator during that time period, and will file a motion for exoneration of bond. The Status on Filing Final Fiduciary Tax Returns and the Order to Appear are Off Calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Joseph. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Curran for Platte River Insurance Company.

Mr. Curran confirmed that the reserve has been paid out to the appropriate people, stated that he does not know if the tax returns have been submitted, that he has no information on the taxes and that he can check with the IRS. Mr. Curran also stated that that someone with authority will have to look into it and that he will have to have his firm appointed as special administrator. The Court instructed Counsel to come in ex parte and stated that it will not require filing of a petition for general appointment. Status on filing of an ex parte petition for appointment of special administrator and status on filing of final fiduciary tax returns are continued to October 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Mr. Curran also stated that he has to subpoena bank records and asked for 60-days to determine if the funds are still there. Status on filing of an ex parte application for appointment of special administrator and status on distribution of the reserve are continued to July 11, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Continued To Apr-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona, counsel for the beneficiary, Debra Simms; James Curran, counsel for Platte River Insurance Co., the bonding agent for the Trustee, Christine Witherspoon.

Mr. Lachona reports on the matters of concern to his client. Mr. Curran advises that the tax returns for the estate have been prepared and will be finalized presently. He anticipates that there will be a small distribution with the Court noting that the reserve account is the only remaining asset. Mr. Curran states that the bonding company anticipates filing for special administration to complete the closing of the estate, rather than seek appointment as a successor personal representative to expedite the closing. The Court advises Mr. Curran to contact Sandy Hilton prior to filing the Petition for Special Administration due to the procedural anomaly of filing the special administration petition without the underlying petition for appointment of administrator.

After consideration, the Court continues the matter to April 11, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for further status although the Court strongly encourages Mr. Curran to file the special administration petition soon.

Judge: Hon. Peter J. Busch; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Jaime; Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Continued To Mar-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Continued To Mar-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Continued To Jan-31-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon (Administrator).
James Curran for Platte River Insurance Company.
Kevin Lachona for Debra Simms (Beneficiary).

Ms. Ng stated that she has had no contact with Ms. Witherspoon and that she still does not know what happened to the reserve. Mr. Lachona stated that he has been trying to contact his Client to see if she wants to petition to be Special Administrator, and requested time to get a hold of her and get a game plan. Mr. Curran stated that he would like to see this wrapped as soon as possible to minimize further expenses, and requested a 60-day continuance for Mr. Lachona to work on his plan and to see if he (Mr. Curran) can work with Ms. Witherspoon and obtain records. Status on distribution of the reserve and filing of the tax returns, and the order to appear/show cause re: surcharge are continued to March 14, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Continued To Jan-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (executor) Christine Witherspoon.

The Court asked if the tax returns have been filed. Ms. Ng replied that she does not know and that there has been no response from Ms. Witherspoon. The Court asked counsel if she can get the records. Ms. Ng replied not unless Ms. Witherspoon gives them to her, that she has gone to her office but the door does not get answered, that as far as a family member stepping in there is no one in a position to do so, that she has not heard from the beneficiaries lately, that the order for final distribution was issued on January 14, 2015, that Ms. Witherspoon was bonded, that final discharge cannot be done without the reserve, and that there has been no response to her attempts to contact Ms. Witherspoon. The Court asked counsel if she is able to check with the tax bureau. Ms. Ng stated that the IRS will not release information to her. The Court stated that it can set a surcharge hearing requiring Ms. Witherspoon to appear and that it can create a basis for relief. The Court continued the status on distribution of reserve to January 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204, that it will issue an Order to Appear to Ms. Witherspoon to show cause regarding the surcharge of the entire amount of the reserve.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Ideta. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (administrator) Christine Witherspoon. Kevin Lachona for (heir) Debra Simms.

The court stated that we are here for a petition for final distribution including a request for fees and other matters and expressed concern about the proposed order assigning a portion of distribution to the law firm instead of a particular amount. Mr. Lachona stated that he filed a declaration this morning addressing this issue and the other issues in the examiner's notes and provided an endorsed copy for the court to review, citing probate code 11604 as guidance for the assignment and expressed that his client is aware of the request. The court stated that a specific assignment from Ms. Simms will be required. The Court granted the petition regarding accounting subject to receipt of a specific assignment from Ms. Simms regarding the amount being assigned to the firm.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lelu. (Not Reported)

The sale was confirmed to Aimee Huang on behalf of her Client John Dai at $560,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $105,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jones. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-15-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Chistine Witherspoon (Petitioner). Jason Ross for Emanuel Sims.

Mr. Ross stated that they are withdrawing their petition and consenting to the appointment of Christine Witherspoon. Mr. Ross also stated that they are withdrawing their objection of Debra Sims being the spouse. Ms. Ng stated that there is a nomination by Debra Sims as to the appointment of Christine Witherspon. Mr. Ross spoke of the family vacating the property in January. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Christine Witherspoon with full authority (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $500,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Apr-08-2013 Continued To May-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emanuel Simms (Petitioner) in Propria Persona. Debra Simms (Petitioner) in Propria Persona. Deanna Simms, Matthew Simms, Marie Simms, Leslie Simms, Cassandra Simms and Clarrissa Simms were also present in Propria Persona.

The Court indicated that there are some procedural issues with all of the petitions, stated that it would be helpful for someone to obtain a Lawyer to assist them and raised the issue of the Parties participating in mediation. In addition, the Court advised the Parties that there is no paper work before it to address the issue of Debra's marriage, asked about having the Public Administrator step in to address the number of legal issues to be decided and made it clear that all Parties would have to agree on the Public Administrator. Debra Simms stated that she thinks she is going to obtain a Probate Lawyer to help her. Both Petitions for Letters of Adminisrtation were continued to May 13, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

The Court informed all Parties that all of the Heirs needed to be given notice and that the issue of whether Debra Simms is the surviving Wife needs to be set for an evidentiary hearing. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to February 27, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for all Heirs to be given notice and for Debra Simms to file a competing petition for appointment of Administrator.
